question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
# `charmcraft.yaml` for a Kubernetes Charm

Here's a complete example with all required fields and common optional sections:

```yaml
# Metadata (required)
name: my-app
type: charm
title: My Application
summary: A short summary of the charm.
description: |
  A longer description of what this charm does,
  how it works, and what it deploys.

# Build configuration (required)
base: ubuntu@24.04
platforms:
  amd64:

# Charm source configuration (required for Kubernetes charms)
parts:
  charm:
    plugin: charm
    source: .

# Kubernetes charm must specify containers (required)
containers:
  my-app:
    resource: my-app-image

# OCI image resources (required — one per container)
resources:
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for my-app
    upstream-source: docker.io/myorg/my-app:latest

# Relations / integrations (optional but very common)
provides:
  grafana-dashboard:
    interface: grafana_dashboard
  metrics-endpoint:
    interface: prometheus_scrape

requires:
  database:
    interface: postgresql_client
    limit: 1
  ingress:
    interface: ingress

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my_app_replica

# Charm configuration options (optional)
config:
  options:
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: info
      description: >
        Logging level (debug, info, warning, error).
    port:
      type: int
      default: 8080
      description: The port the application listens on.
    enable-tls:
      type: boolean
      default: false
      description: Whether to enable TLS.

# Juju actions (optional)
actions:
  backup:
    description: Create a backup of the application data.
    params:
      destination:
        type: string
        description: Backup destination path.
    required:
      - destination
  restart:
    description: Restart the application.

# Storage (optional)
storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    location: /data
    minimum-size: 1G

# Assumes (optional — declare minimum Juju features needed)
assumes:
  - juju >= 3.1
  - k8s-api
```

## Key Required Fields Breakdown

| Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `name` | Unique charm name (lowercase, hyphens) |
| `type` | Must be `charm` |
| `summary` | One-line description |
| `description` | Multi-line description |
| `base` | Target OS (e.g. `ubuntu@22.04`, `ubuntu@24.04`) |
| `platforms` | Target architectures (`amd64`, `arm64`) |
| `parts.charm` | Build instructions — `plugin: charm` for ops charms |
| `containers` | **What makes it a Kubernetes charm** — at least one container |
| `resources` | OCI images referenced by containers |

## Key Differences from Machine Charms

- **Kubernetes charms** have a `containers:` section — machine charms do not.
- Each container references a `resource` of `type: oci-image`.
- Kubernetes charms use Pebble to manage workloads inside containers.

## Notes

- **`base`** replaces the older `bases:` list syntax (Charmcraft 3.x+). For older Charmcraft 2.x, you'd use `bases: [{ name: ubuntu, channel: "22.04" }]`.
- **`platforms`** replaces the older `architectures` syntax.
- **`provides` / `requires` / `peers`** define relation endpoints with their interface names.
- **`assumes`** lets Juju check prerequisites before deployment.
